drug_interactions.description: Lisinopril may increase risk of hyperkalemia when combined with Atorvastatin. Monitor potassium levels.
Lisinopril combined with Prednisone may reduce antihypertensive effect. Monitor blood pressure.
Atorvastatin levels may increase when combined with Amlodipine. Consider dose adjustment.
Amoxicillin may reduce absorption of Gabapentin. Separate doses by 2 hours.
SEVERE: Lisinopril combined with Gabapentin significantly increases risk of hyperkalemia and hypotension. Avoid combination if possible.
Amlodipine may slightly increase Sertraline levels. Usually not clinically significant.
Atorvastatin combined with Losartan may increase risk of hyperkalemia. Monitor levels.
SEVERE: Levothyroxine combined with Warfarin may significantly increase anticoagulant effect. Adjust doses carefully.
Metformin combined with Tramadol may increase risk of gastrointestinal side effects.
SEVERE: Atorvastatin combined with Clopidogrel may reduce antiplatelet effect. Consider alternative statin.
Lisinopril combined with Tramadol may increase risk of hypotension. Monitor blood pressure.
